description: External survey of cooperative allocation mechanisms: UCB1, Shapley value, DARPA model, portfolio diversification, open source allocation, anti-competitive economics
expect: External mechanisms provide principled replacement for swarm's ad-hoc dispatch scoring (10+ tuning constants)

ucb1_replacement.replaces: HEAT_DECAY, HEAT_PENALTY_MAX, DORMANT_BONUS, FIRST_VISIT_BONUS, COOLDOWN_SESSIONS, COOLDOWN_MAX_PENALTY, VISIT_SATURATION_SCALE, EXPLORATION_GINI_THRESHOLD, EXPLORATION_NEW_BOOST, EXPLORATION_COLD_BOOST
ucb1_replacement.single_parameter: c controls exploration-exploitation (c=2.0 is standard UCB1)
ucb1_replacement.guarantee: logarithmic regret O(log t) — provably optimal exploration

thompson_sampling_alternative.formula: sample from Beta(alpha + successes, beta + failures) per domain
thompson_sampling_alternative.advantage: handles non-stationarity (domain yields change), integrates prior knowledge
thompson_sampling_alternative.implementation: maintain Beta posteriors per domain, use structural features as priors

shapley_value.concept: each domain's marginal contribution across all possible dispatch coalitions
shapley_value.advantage: replaces structural feature scoring with empirical marginal yield measurement
shapley_value.tractability: 25 domains tractable via sampling-based Shapley approximation
shapley_value.replicable_resources_extension: 2025 paper extends Shapley to non-rivalrous resources (exactly swarm's situation)

darpa_hard_floor.mechanism: at least 20% of dispatches to domains with <3 prior visits regardless of score
darpa_hard_floor.rationale: DARPA allocates ≥20% to new investments; forces turnover, prevents exploitation lock-in
darpa_hard_floor.simpler_than: multi-layered penalty system currently in use

portfolio_diversification_for_dispatch.markowitz_for_research: optimal portfolio balances expected return against inter-domain correlation
portfolio_diversification_for_dispatch.finding: penalize portfolios where selected domains have highly correlated outcomes
portfolio_diversification_for_dispatch.advantage: targets mechanism (correlated yields waste capacity) not symptom (unequal visit counts)

open_source_models.apache_do_ocracy: work claimed by doing, not allocated centrally — swarm's claim.py is a primitive version
open_source_models.wikipedia_stigmergy: indirect coordination via environmental signals (cleanup tags, quality categories) not central allocation
open_source_models.linux_kernel: subsystem maintainer model — tree of responsibility, pull-based integration
open_source_models.key_insight: ASF/Wikipedia do NOT allocate centrally; they surface signals and let contributors self-select — dispatch_optimizer is anti-pattern

proportional_fairness.alpha_fairness: alpha=0 exploitation, alpha=1 proportional, alpha→∞ max-min; single parameter generalizes
proportional_fairness.advantage: concavity of log naturally penalizes concentration

ambidexterity.finding: organizations need structural separation between exploration and exploitation units
ambidexterity.different_metrics: exploration measured by discovery rate; exploitation by efficiency
ambidexterity.swarm_application: classify domains as exploration/exploitation, apply different success metrics

theoretical_validation.why_explicit_scores_worsened_coverage: consistent with MAB literature: naive exploitation of estimated rewards (greedy policy) provably leads to suboptimal outcomes because it fails to account for uncertainty
theoretical_validation.UCB_and_Thompson_solve_this: uncertainty itself is a reason to explore — current cooldown/saturation are heuristic patches for a formally solved problem

tier_1_drop_in: DARPA hard exploration floor (20% to cold domains)

diff: Expected cooperative allocation mechanisms to exist — confirmed across multiple fields. Did NOT expect a single-parameter replacement (UCB1 c) for 10+ tuning constants — the current system's complexity is formally unnecessary. DARPA 20% floor is simpler than all current patches. Open source models (ASF/Wikipedia) suggest dispatch optimizer itself may be an anti-pattern — signaling + self-selection instead of centralized scoring. The theoretical insight is the most important: explicit scores worsening coverage is a KNOWN FAILURE MODE of greedy policies in MAB literature.
